persons.arguments_against: Counter-hypotheses: (1) one of the other three Haddon-Smith officials in the corpus held this stint; (2) the position_sim FAIL signals a real position mismatch; (3) a same-name relative succeeded him as governor. All fail. The stint is for the 'Governor and Commander-in-Chief of the Windward Islands' styled 'Sir George B. Haddon-Smith, K.C.M.G.' — the biography records exactly 'gov., Windward Is., 14th Dec., 1914' and a K.C.M.G. conferred 1915, the very year the stint begins; the forename initials 'George B.' match 'Geo. Babil' precisely. The position_sim FAIL is a mechanical artifact (timeline cell holds only the abbreviation 'gov.'); read verbatim, the offices are identical. The stint window 1915-1925 matches the bio's tenure (appointed Dec 1914, 'ret., 1924.', with retiree listing into 1925). No other Haddon-Smith could plausibly be a knighted Windward Islands governor with the same forename and honour in those years.

evidence_cited.fact: Governor and Commander-in-Chief of the Windward Islands

persons.would_overturn: Discovery of a second contemporaneous George B. Haddon-Smith, K.C.M.G., or records showing a different person held the Windward Islands governorship in 1915-1924 while this biography subject served elsewhere.

notes: Position match is exact when read from verbatim text (Governor and C-in-C, Windward Islands); the deterministic position_sim FAIL compares only the abbreviated token 'gov.'. Honour K.C.M.G. (1915) aligns with the stint's first listed year. Stint listings end 1925, consistent with 'ret., 1924.'.
